message:
fix(og): add missing slack-mattermost-migration-twitter.jpg

Commit 6c77673 added five of the six pre-rendered share-card JPEGs but
the twitter variant for slack-mattermost-migration was not regenerated
in that run — the local Next.js dev server hung on that specific route
during the prerender pass while the other five completed normally. Prod
renders the same route fine, so this JPEG was produced by pulling the
live PNG from jeffreyemanuel.com/writing/slack-mattermost-migration/
twitter-image and running it through the same sharp pipeline the
prerender script uses (mozjpeg quality=85, progressive). Output is
deterministic from the dynamic route, so the visual result is identical
to running the script cleanly.

Without this file, the article page's `twitter:image` meta tag points at
a 404, which is exactly the broken-share state the prerender pipeline is
supposed to prevent on X. Shipping it restores the full card on that
article. Running `bun run prerender:og` going forward should regenerate
all six JPEGs without needing a manual prod pull.

Dimensions: 1200x600, progressive JPEG, 93KB.
